Article Name: Aspirin can reduce the risk of cancer - and we're starting to understand why
Key Summary, Notes, and Takeaways: A man who's whole family has faced bowel cancer participated in a clinical trial to test if a dose of aspirin would protect against the cancer. There have been many studies across the world over the last 15 years that have worked to prove that Apirin has reduced both the incidence and spread of cancer. It is unclear if this will work for all cancers, but it has had many positive impacts and in one UK study they found that the Aspirin helps the cancer cells become more visible to the immune system.
